title: Understanding Centripetal Force
text: Centripetal force is the force that keeps an object moving in a circular path. It acts towards the center of the circle.

title: Formula for Centripetal Force
text: The formula for centripetal force \(F_c\) is given by \(F_c=m*v**2/r\), where \(m\) is the mass, \(v\) is the velocity, and \(r\) is the radius.

title: Convert Revolutions per Minute to Meters per Second
text: To find the velocity \(v\), convert the angular speed from revolutions per minute to meters per second. Use the formula \(v=2*pi*r*(revolutions/minute)/60\).

title: Calculate the Velocity
text: Given the radius $$\(r=1.25\)$$ $$m$$ and the angular speed \(40\) $$\frac{rev}{min}$$, calculate the velocity \(v\).

title: Calculate the Centripetal Force
text: Now that you have the velocity, use the formula \(F_c=m*v**2/r\) to calculate the centripetal force.
